name: review_status
description: Check the current Monocle review status. Returns whether feedback is pending, a pause was requested, or no feedback is available.

name: get_feedback
description: Retrieve review feedback from Monocle. With wait=true, blocks until the reviewer submits feedback.

name: send_artifact
description: Send content to Monocle for the reviewer to see. Provide content directly or pass file_path to read from disk.

name: add_files
description: Add file paths to the current Monocle review session.

name: remove_files
description: Remove previously-added file paths from the current Monocle review session. Pass the same paths used with add_files.

name: set_file_groups
description: Organize the changed files in Monocle's grouped sidebar view for the reviewer. Provide entries, each with a path (matching a changed file from review_status) and optional grouping metadata: workstream (optional top-level grouping above the group — use it when the change spans multiple independent efforts/features, e.g. two parallel workstreams, so files nest as workstream → group → files; omit it for a single-workstream change), workstream_order (integer; lower workstreams shown first), group (free-form label like "UI", "Backend", "Database" to tell the change as a top-down story), group_order (integer; lower groups shown first), sort_index (order within a group; lower first), category (one of code/test/config/docs/build to override the path heuristic), and criticality (integer; higher = more important). Order workstreams and groups from entry-point to dependency (e.g. UI → API → database) so the reviewer reads the change in flow order. Set replace=true to replace all prior grouping. The reviewer presses 'f' to cycle to the grouped view.

name: add_annotations
description: Attach short rationale notes to specific ranges of the changed code to guide the reviewer, each linking out to the docs that motivate it. These annotations are shown to the reviewer only — they are NOT review feedback and are never sent back to you. Provide entries: file (a changed-file path from review_status), line_start and line_end (the exact code range the note explains), summary (one line: why this code exists / what it does), and refs — an array of doc links, each with kind ('file' for a repo path or 'artifact' for a sent artifact id), doc (the path or artifact id), label (link text), and a line/column range into that doc (start_line, start_col, end_line, end_col; 1-based lines, 0-based cols) pointing at the relevant passage in a TODO or design doc. Keep summaries to one line; attach multiple refs when several docs apply. Set replace=true to clear all prior annotations first (default replaces per file). The reviewer clicks or keys through the refs to open the doc passage beside the code. The response reports how many were accepted and lists any rejected entries with reasons (e.g. line_end < line_start, or a file that isn't in the review) plus warnings for doc refs that don't resolve — read it and resend corrected entries. Annotations are cleared automatically when the reviewer submits and the round advances, so re-annotate against the new code each round.

name: set_review_name
description: Give the current review a short, human-friendly name shown in Monocle's top bar (e.g. 'Add OAuth login' or 'Payments refactor'). Call this once when you start a review so the reviewer can see at a glance what they're reviewing. Pass name (a brief title); an empty name clears it.
